title: The Wildest Sun A Novel
author: Asha Lemmie
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Wildest Sun A Novel" book by Asha Lemmie?
Options:
A. My mother was right about many things—God knows I wish I could tell her that—but she was also the architect of her own misery. I’m not afraid of Paris because of her. I know that I don’t have to be like her. I have started something here in Havana and I know in my bones that it isn’t finished. I think that perhaps Paris was intended to be the home of my childhood, but not the home to whatever it is that I am becoming. We are not all destined to grow in our native soil, you told me that yourself. So now I am blooming under a different sun. 
B. My mother was right regarding numerous things—God understands I desire to inform her of that—but she also built her own unhappiness. I do not fear Paris due to her. I know that I need not emulate her. I have initiated something here in Havana and I know intrinsically that it is not concluded. I believe that perhaps Paris was meant to be the house of my childhood, however not the house to whatever I am developing into. We are not all predestined to mature in our native soil, you told me that yourself. Thus now I am flourishing under an alternative sunlight.
C. My mother was correct regarding numerous things—the Lord knows I desire to inform her of that—but she also constructed her own unhappiness. I do not fear Paris due to her. I understand that I need not imitate her. I have commenced something here in Havana and I know intrinsically that it is incomplete. I believe that perhaps Paris was meant to be the residence of my childhood, however not the residence to whatever I am transforming into. We are not all fated to mature in our native land, you informed me of that yourself. Thus now I am flourishing under an alternate sunlight.
D. My mother was accurate about many matters—heaven knows I long to tell her that—but she too was the designer of her own misery. I'm not scared of Paris because of her. I comprehend that I don't have to be similar to her. I have started something here in Havana and I know deep down that it's not over. I think that maybe Paris was supposed to be the home of my childhood, but not the home to whatever I am becoming. We are not all destined to grow where we were born, you told me that yourself. So now I am blooming under a different sun.  
Answer:

options.A: My mother was right about many things—God knows I wish I could tell her that—but she was also the architect of her own misery. I’m not afraid of Paris because of her. I know that I don’t have to be like her. I have started something here in Havana and I know in my bones that it isn’t finished. I think that perhaps Paris was intended to be the home of my childhood, but not the home to whatever it is that I am becoming. We are not all destined to grow in our native soil, you told me that yourself. So now I am blooming under a different sun.
options.B: My mother was right regarding numerous things—God understands I desire to inform her of that—but she also built her own unhappiness. I do not fear Paris due to her. I know that I need not emulate her. I have initiated something here in Havana and I know intrinsically that it is not concluded. I believe that perhaps Paris was meant to be the house of my childhood, however not the house to whatever I am developing into. We are not all predestined to mature in our native soil, you told me that yourself. Thus now I am flourishing under an alternative sunlight.
options.C: My mother was correct regarding numerous things—the Lord knows I desire to inform her of that—but she also constructed her own unhappiness. I do not fear Paris due to her. I understand that I need not imitate her. I have commenced something here in Havana and I know intrinsically that it is incomplete. I believe that perhaps Paris was meant to be the residence of my childhood, however not the residence to whatever I am transforming into. We are not all fated to mature in our native land, you informed me of that yourself. Thus now I am flourishing under an alternate sunlight.
options.D: My mother was accurate about many matters—heaven knows I long to tell her that—but she too was the designer of her own misery. I'm not scared of Paris because of her. I comprehend that I don't have to be similar to her. I have started something here in Havana and I know deep down that it's not over.
